body.title: [sprint] Provider abstraction with billing types + legacy model-ID -> capability map
body.intention: I intend that the inference engine knows each provider's billing type and can map any model ID callers actually emit to a capability tier, so routing is general across anyone's provider mix.
body.description:
**Problem:** sources.py conflates provider with everything else; there's no billing-type concept and no map from the concrete model IDs callers actually emit (anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, llama3, openai/gpt-4o-mini) to a capability tier. Without that map the router (T-inference-policy-router) can't route real traffic and Igor stays mute.

**Shape:** Give each Source a `billing` type: local (owned hardware, free at margin), subscription (flat-rate), usage (per-token, metered). Register the real set: ollama_cloud=subscription (primary), google_free=usage, local_ollama=local. Add a models registry that maps every legacy/concrete model ID (incl. the three above) -> a capability tier; unknown ID -> a sane default tier. Liveness reports honestly (local_ollama's current 404 -> unavailable, NOT an error that fails the build).

**Affected files:** devices/inference/sources.py (billing on Source + registry), devices/inference/models_registry.py (legacy-ID->tier), devices/inference/self_test_sources.py, default_registry().
**Design rules:** inference-proxy-only; general (billing types, not provider names); honest liveness; no-creds-in-source.
**Scope boundary:** IN — billing-type on providers, register the 3 working sources, legacy-ID->tier map (unknown->default), honest liveness. OUT — the router (T-inference-policy-router), the cost gate (T-inference-usage-cost-gate).
**Test plan:** providers expose a billing type; models_registry maps anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, llama3, openai/gpt-4o-mini AND an unknown ID to a tier; self_test passes for AVAILABLE providers and reports a down local as unavailable (does not fail).
**Completion criteria:** `default_registry()` lists each provider with a billing type; models_registry resolves the 3 named legacy IDs + unknown->default to a capability tier; self-test green for reachable providers with local-404 reported unavailable (not a failure).
